description: Update malformed `dms.EffectiveSchema` runtime validation so `DatabaseFingerprintReaderSupport` preserves every contract issue returned by `EffectiveSchemaFingerprintContract.GetStoredValidationIssues(...)` instead of discarding all but the first one. Acceptance: a malformed fingerprint row with multiple invalid fields produces a deterministic ordered set of issues, single-issue failures still behave the same, and existing projection/null/type/multiple-row validation paths continue to throw `DatabaseFingerprintValidationException` without regression.
steps-to-verify: Add or update backend unit tests covering a malformed fingerprint row with multiple validation issues and a single-issue regression case.
Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter DatabaseFingerprintReaderSupportTests`

description: Update `ValidateDatabaseFingerprintMiddleware` to surface every malformed fingerprint issue in the `errors` array of the 503 problem response, while keeping the existing remediation guidance in `detail`. Acceptance: the response contains each validation issue in order plus the restart/remediation guidance, existing malformed-fingerprint logging remains intact, and current single-issue responses keep their existing shape apart from the expanded `errors` payload.
steps-to-verify: Add or update middleware unit tests for a cached `DatabaseFingerprintValidationException` containing multiple issues and verify the 503 problem details payload.

Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter ValidateDatabaseFingerprintMiddlewareValidationErrorTests`

description: Introduce SQL Server integration-test coverage infrastructure for backend fingerprint reader tests. Acceptance: there is a dedicated MSSQL integration test project or equivalent test harness in the backend area, it is added to the DMS solution, it can obtain SQL Server test configuration using repository conventions, and it can provision the core `dms.*` DDL needed for `dms.EffectiveSchema` reader tests.
steps-to-verify: Create the MSSQL integration test project/harness and confirm it builds under the DMS solution.

Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Mssql.Tests.Integration/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Mssql.Tests.Integration.csproj --filter FullyQualifiedName~MssqlDatabaseFingerprintReaderTests`

description: Add `MssqlDatabaseFingerprintReader` runtime integration tests against a real SQL Server database. Acceptance: the test provisions core DDL, inserts a valid `dms.EffectiveSchema` singleton row, reads it through `MssqlDatabaseFingerprintReader`, and asserts all four cached fingerprint fields (`ApiSchemaFormatVersion`, `EffectiveSchemaHash`, `ResourceKeyCount`, `ResourceKeySeedHash`) match the inserted values.
steps-to-verify: Mirror the existing PostgreSQL runtime reader scenario for MSSQL using actual `SqlConnection`/`SqlDataReader` behavior.

Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Mssql.Tests.Integration/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Mssql.Tests.Integration.csproj --filter FullyQualifiedName~MssqlDatabaseFingerprintReaderTests`

description: Extend pipeline ordering coverage to include `CreateGetTokenInfoPipeline`. Acceptance: unit tests assert that `ValidateDatabaseFingerprintMiddleware` is present in the token-info pipeline and executes before both `ApiSchemaValidationMiddleware` and `GetTokenInfoHandler`, while still allowing the token-info pipeline to omit `ParsePathMiddleware` by design.
steps-to-verify: Add or extend pipeline ordering tests to inspect the private token-info pipeline in `ApiService` using the same approach already used for the query pipeline.
Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter PipelineOrderingTests`

description: Align `ResourceKeyCount` to the authoritative `smallint`/`short` effective-schema contract across runtime and provisioning by changing `EffectiveSchemaInfo.ResourceKeyCount` and dependent builders, fixtures, and tests to match `DatabaseFingerprint` and the provisioned `dms.EffectiveSchema` table. Acceptance: `EffectiveSchemaInfo`, runtime fingerprint handling, provisioning helpers, and DDL seed emission no longer rely on an `int` to `short` narrowing for `ResourceKeyCount`, and affected backend/test projects build and execute against the aligned contract without regression.
steps-to-verify: Update the compile-time contracts, builders, fixtures, and tests so the effective-schema model no longer exposes `int ResourceKeyCount` in the backend external contract.
Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter DatabaseFingerprintReaderSupportTests`
Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Ddl.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Ddl.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter SeedDmlEmitterTests`

description: Add explicit shared validation and regression coverage for the `ResourceKeyCount` `smallint` ceiling so malformed effective-schema metadata fails with a deterministic contract message before overflow or provider-specific cast behavior. Acceptance: counts above `short.MaxValue` are rejected by shared validation, invalid expected metadata no longer first fails at `checked((short)...)`, boundary-valid values still succeed, and new tests cover both over-limit and boundary-success cases.
steps-to-verify: Add unit tests for over-limit and boundary `ResourceKeyCount` values in the shared effective-schema validation/emission paths.
Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Ddl.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Ddl.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter SeedDmlEmitterTests`
Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/clis/EdFi.DataManagementService.SchemaTools.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.SchemaTools.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter SeedValidatorTests`

description: Align backend redesign documentation for `ResourceKeyCount` with the implemented `dms.EffectiveSchema` contract. Acceptance: `compiled-mapping-set.md` and any related runtime-mapping-selection documentation no longer describe `ResourceKeyCount` as an unconstrained `int` when the authoritative storage/runtime contract is `smallint`-bounded, and the capacity rationale is stated explicitly so reviewers can see why the runtime model uses the tighter limit.
steps-to-verify: Update the affected design docs so `ResourceKeyCount` uses consistent type/capacity language everywhere it is described.

Run: `rg -n "ResourceKeyCount" reference/design/backend-redesign/design-docs reference/design/backend-redesign/epics/06-runtime-mapping-selection` and confirm the updated docs are internally consistent.

description: Add a usage comment clarifying when middleware should use `ProblemDetailsResponse` versus `FailureResponse`. Acceptance: `ProblemDetailsResponse` documents that it is the middleware-oriented factory for complete typed `FrontendResponse` problem responses, the comment distinguishes it from the legacy JSON-body `FailureResponse` helper, any related note in handler utilities is updated to avoid implying the helpers are interchangeable, and no runtime behavior changes.
steps-to-verify: Update the summary or remarks in `ProblemDetailsResponse.cs` and adjust nearby explanatory comments if needed so the distinction is explicit in code review.
Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter ProblemDetailsResponseTests`
